questions.text: I am careful to double-check my work for errors before submitting it.
I find that most things that upset other people register as mild inconveniences for me.
I trust that I can handle whatever comes up, so I don't waste time on contingency plans.
I often need someone to explain the social dynamics of a situation after the fact.
I am cautious about overruling others, even when the stakes are high.
I am drawn to situations where my voice carries significant weight.
I hold the view that I deserve the rewards of my own hard work.
I rarely notice when someone is performing a social role rather than being authentic.
I feel that my personal ambitions should take a back seat to the well-being of those I care about.
I find that formalizing my goals can make them feel like obligations rather than aspirations.
I find that I am most trusted in difficult situations because I bring logical clarity rather than emotional intensity.
I detect shifts in a group's mood almost instantly.
I tend to keep my best ideas for my own projects rather than giving them away.
I find that I am at my most helpful when I can analyze a crisis without being pulled into its emotional current.
I hold the view that the pull toward responding to suffering is the most honest expression of the values I actually hold.
I believe my steady emotional tempo is something people can count on about me.
I feel empowered when the group trusts me enough to share power.
My emotional responses have a vividness that makes everyday life feel dramatic.
